steps.description: LixMoS2 was prepared as described in ref. [4]. Reaction of LixMoS2 with D2O results in the formation of completely exfoliated single layers of MoS2.

For the restacked MoS2 sample (r=0, no macrocycles), the exfoliated MoS2 solution in D2O was processed without adding macrocycles. The reaction mixture was allowed to stir at room temperature for two days.
The mixture was filtered and the insoluble product washed thoroughly with D2O to remove LiOH and excess macrocycles.

synthesis.notes: This is the restacked MoS2 sample without macrocycles (r=0). The composition (D2O)0.08MoS2 was determined by thermogravimetric analysis (TGA) using a TA 500 instrument. The intercalation was carried out in deuterated water (D2O) to facilitate neutron scattering measurements. LixMoS2 preparation is described in ref. [4] (Bissessur et al., J. Mater. Chem. 13, 44, 2003).

steps.description: LixMoS2 was reacted with D2O to form completely exfoliated single layers of MoS2.

A solution of the macrocycles in D2O was added to the exfoliated MoS2 solution. The reaction mixture was allowed to stir at room temperature for two days.
The mixture was filtered and the insoluble product was washed thoroughly with D2O to remove LiOH and excess macrocycles.
The insoluble product was washed thoroughly with D2O to remove LiOH and excess macrocycles.

synthesis.notes: Deuterated macrocycles were used to enable neutron scattering measurements that highlight the macrocycles relative to the MoS2 sheets. The macrocycle loading r and water content z were determined by thermogravimetric analysis. A series of compounds with varying r (from 0 to ~0.099) was prepared.

steps.description: Prepare a solution of the deuterated macrocycles in D2O.
Add the macrocycle solution to the exfoliated MoS2 solution.
Stir the reaction mixture at room temperature for two days.
Filter the mixture to collect the insoluble product.
Wash the product thoroughly with D2O to remove LiOH and excess macrocycles.
